parentQuestion: Sympathetic collateral ganglia (prevertebral ganglia) are located anterior to the vertebral column.

scaffolds.q: The sympathetic system has two types of ganglia: Paravertebral and prevertebral (collateral).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Paravertebral ganglia form the sympathetic chain alongside the spine. Prevertebral ganglia are separate clusters further forward.

scaffolds.q: Paravertebral ganglia form a chain ALONGSIDE (lateral to) the vertebral column.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The sympathetic trunk (chain ganglia) runs parallel to the spine on both sides, like two strings of beads.

scaffolds.q: 'Prevertebral' means in FRONT of the vertebral column.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! 'Pre-' means before/in front of. Prevertebral ganglia are located anterior to (in front of) the vertebral column.

scaffolds.q: Collateral ganglia are another name for prevertebral ganglia.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Same structures, different names. Collateral/prevertebral ganglia include celiac, superior mesenteric, and inferior mesenteric.

scaffolds.q: Collateral ganglia cluster around major abdominal arteries.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The celiac ganglion surrounds the celiac trunk. The mesenteric ganglia are near the superior and inferior mesenteric arteries.

scaffolds.q: These ganglia innervate abdominal and pelvic viscera.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Postganglionic fibers from collateral ganglia follow blood vessels to reach GI tract, kidneys, bladder, and reproductive organs.

scaffolds.q: Splanchnic nerves carry preganglionic fibers to collateral ganglia.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Splanchnic nerves (greater, lesser, least) carry sympathetic preganglionic fibers that pass through the chain WITHOUT synapsing to reach collateral ganglia.

scaffolds.q: Collateral (prevertebral) ganglia are located anterior to the vertebral column.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! They're in front of the spine, around the abdominal aorta - hence 'prevertebral.'

scaffolds.q: The statement is TRUE - collateral ganglia are anterior to the vertebral column.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The answer is TRUE. Collateral ganglia (celiac, mesenteric) are prevertebral = in front of the vertebral column, around the major abdominal arteries.
